zh.Fordington, Dorset, England: 英國英格蘭多瑟特福丁頓
zh.Newton Abbot, Devon, England: 英國戴文牛頓 阿博特
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield於1470年出生在英國戴文艾可謝特。她與Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court在英國英格蘭多瑟特福丁頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1530年在英國戴文牛頓 阿博特享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield于1470年出生在英国戴文艾可謝特。她与Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court在英国英格兰多瑟特福丁頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1530年在英国戴文牛頓 阿博特享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Banfield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Banville in Calvados, Normandy, France. There has been some confusion between this surname and   Bonfield  . In Ireland this is a variant of   Banville  , the original French form of the placename, and now the usual spelling of the surname in Ireland; it is associated primarily with County Wexford.

English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places in England with similar names, such as Bampfylde House in Poltimore, Devon and Bamville Farm in Wheathampstead, Hertfordshire. There may have been some confusion with   Benfield  .

variants.name: Banville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Alban, Andre, Armand, Benoit.
French and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Banville in Calvados, France, recorded in the 13th century as Badanvilla ‘estate of a woman called Bada’. Compare Banfield .

variants.name: Bonfield
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name, altered by folk etymology, from any of the three places in northern France called Bonneville, from Old French bonne ‘good’ + ville ‘settlement’. There has been some confusion with Banfield .

variants.name: Banfill
meanings: English (Surrey) and Irish: variant of   Banfield  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield was born in 1470, in Exeter, Devon, England. She married Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court in Fordington, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1530, in Newton Abbot, Devon, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield est née en 1470, à Exeter, Devon, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court à Fordington, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1530, à Newton Abbot, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield wurde 1470 in Exeter, Devon, England geboren. Sie hat in Fordington, Dorset, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1530 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Newton Abbot, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield è nata il nel 1470 nel Exeter, Devon, England. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court nel Fordington, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1530, nel Newton Abbot, Devon, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfieldは1470年にイギリスデボンエクセターで生まれた。イギリスイギリスドールセトフォーディントンでSir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Courtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1530年にイギリスデボンニュートン アボットで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield(은)는 1470년 영국 데폰 세터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 포딩턴에서 Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1530년 영국 데폰 뉴턴 애벗에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield nasceu em 1470, em Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court em Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1530, em Newton Abbot, Devon, Inglaterra, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield родилась в 1470 г. в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court в местности фордингтон, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1530 г. в местности нютон эббот, девон, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Margaret Banfield nació en 1470, en Exeter, Devon, Inglaterra. Se casó con Sir Thomas Alfred Yeard Knight of Denton Court en Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1530, en Newton Abbot, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años.
